task_completion.description: Create a dashboard named 'CPU Budget Allocation'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Graphite web UI shows a dashboard with the exact title 'CPU Budget Allocation' at the top.

task_completion.description: Create the first graph titled 'Instance CPU Share'
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph panel exists in the dashboard with the exact title 'Instance CPU Share'.

task_completion.description: Configure the first graph with asPercent(), sumSeries(), and the three specified EC2 metrics
task_completion.visual_evidence: The metric composer or query editor for the first graph shows the functions 'asPercent' and 'sumSeries' applied to 'servers.ec2_instance_1.cpu.utilization', 'servers.ec2_instance_2.cpu.utilization', and 'servers.ec2_instance_3.cpu.cloudwatch_utilization'.

task_completion.description: Set the first graph to use stacked area/line mode
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Instance CPU Share' graph visually displays as a stacked area or stacked line chart (areas/lines are filled and stacked on top of each other).

task_completion.description: Create the second graph titled 'Fleet CPU Spread'
task_completion.visual_evidence: A second graph panel exists in the dashboard with the exact title 'Fleet CPU Spread'.

task_completion.description: Configure the second graph with rangeOfSeries(), maxSeries(), and minSeries() across the three instance metrics
task_completion.visual_evidence: The metric composer or query editor for the second graph shows the functions 'rangeOfSeries', 'maxSeries', and 'minSeries' applied to the three specified EC2 instance metrics.

integrity.description: The agent used the Graphite web interface to create the dashboard
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Graphite web UI (browser window, menus, graph composer) being interacted with, rather than terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply write the expected dashboard JSON into a text editor
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show genuine interaction with the Graphite dashboard UI, not a text editor (like vim, nano, or gedit) editing a JSON file.

integrity.description: The graphs successfully render actual metric data
integrity.visual_evidence: The graph panels display plotted lines or stacked areas representing real data, rather than empty grids, broken image icons, or error messages.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected dashboard name is exactly 'CPU Budget Allocation'. The first graph must be titled 'Instance CPU Share' and use stacked area/line mode with asPercent() and sumSeries() on the three EC2 metrics. The second graph must be titled 'Fleet CPU Spread' and use rangeOfSeries(), maxSeries(), and minSeries() on the same three metrics. All names and titles are case-sensitive.
